name: page-cro
description: When the user wants to optimize, improve, or increase conversions on any marketing page — including homepage, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, or blog posts. Also use when the user says "CRO," "conversion rate optimization," "this page isn't converting," "improve conversions," or "why isn't this page working." For signup/registration flows, see signup-flow-cro. For post-signup activation, see onboarding-cro. For forms outside of signup, see form-cro. For popups/modals, see popup-cro.
capabilities: Analyzes marketing pages across 7 key dimensions including value proposition, headlines, CTAs, visual hierarchy, trust signals, objection handling, and friction points
Provides page-specific optimization frameworks for homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, and blog posts
Delivers structured recommendations organized as quick wins, high-impact changes, test ideas, and copy alternatives
Identifies specific experiments to test based on page type and conversion goals
author: coreyhaines31
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only marketing CRO guidance in Markdown format. Static scanner flagged common words like "weak" and "traffic" as security patterns, but these are false positives. There is no executable code, no network operations, and no security risk. All findings are dismissed as the file contains only descriptive text about conversion rate optimization techniques.
valueStatement: Marketing pages often fail to convert visitors into customers due to unclear value propositions, weak CTAs, or poor visual hierarchy. This skill provides a systematic framework to analyze pages and deliver actionable CRO recommendations.
limitations: Does not access or analyze live websites directly - works from user descriptions or provided page content
Cannot run A/B tests or measure actual conversion performance
Does not provide quantitative analysis of existing conversion rates or traffic data
Relies on user-provided context about traffic sources and business goals

useCases.title: Improve Landing Page Performance
useCases.description: Analyze a paid traffic landing page to identify why conversions are low. Get specific recommendations for headline changes, CTA improvements, and trust signal placement.

useCases.targetUser: SaaS product managers and growth teams
useCases.title: Optimize Pricing Page Conversion
useCases.description: Review pricing page to increase signup rates. Identify pricing presentation issues, compare feature clarity problems, and find missing objection handling opportunities.

useCases.title: Transform Blog Content into Lead Generator
useCases.description: Optimize blog posts with CTAs that convert readers. Find the best placement for inline CTAs, improve lead magnet offers, and reduce friction in the conversion flow.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Page Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: This is a [homepage/landing page/pricing page/blog post] at [URL or description]. The primary goal is to get visitors to [desired action]. Traffic comes mainly from [traffic source]. Please analyze this page using your CRO framework and give me specific recommendations for improving conversions.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a quick analysis of a marketing page with basic context

promptTemplates.title: Detailed Page Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: I want you to do a comprehensive CRO audit of my [page type]. The page is located at [URL or provide description]. Current conversion rate is [X]%. Target audience is [description]. Main competitors are [list]. Please analyze: 1) Value proposition clarity, 2) Headline effectiveness, 3) CTA placement and copy, 4) Visual hierarchy, 5) Trust signals, 6) Objection handling, 7) Friction points. For each area, identify issues and suggest specific improvements.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a thorough multi-dimensional analysis

promptTemplates.title: Experiment Hypothesis Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: My [landing page/pricing page] is converting at [X]%. I want to run experiments to improve this. Based on best practices for this page type, what are the top 5 experiments I should consider? For each, explain the hypothesis, what to test, and how to measure success.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need A/B testing ideas and hypotheses

promptTemplates.title: Specific Element Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: The main CTA on my [page type] currently says [current copy] and converts at [X]%. The button color is [color]. The CTA appears [location]. Please suggest 5 alternative CTA button copy options with rationale for each, and explain how to prioritize testing them.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want specific alternatives for CTAs, headlines, or other elements

bestPractices: Start analysis by identifying the single primary conversion goal - clarity on this prevents diluting recommendations across multiple objectives
Focus recommendations on above-the-fold elements first - changes here have highest impact on conversion rates
Always provide alternative copy suggestions with rationale - helps users understand the why behind recommendations
antiPatterns: Adding multiple CTAs on a single page - this creates choice overload and reduces conversions
Focusing on features instead of benefits - visitors care about outcomes, not specifications
Ignoring mobile experience in recommendations - many conversions happen on mobile devices

faq.question: What information should I provide for the best page analysis?
faq.answer: Provide the page URL, page type, primary conversion goal, traffic source, and current conversion rate. Any additional context about user research, heatmaps, or what you have already tried helps deliver more relevant recommendations.

faq.question: Does this skill access my website directly?
faq.answer: No. This skill analyzes pages based on your descriptions or provided content. It does not crawl websites, access analytics, or run any automated tests on your pages.

faq.question: How is this different from a general copywriting skill?
faq.answer: This skill focuses specifically on conversion optimization - analyzing existing pages for conversion barriers and providing structured recommendations. Copywriting skills help write new content from scratch. This skill evaluates and improves existing marketing pages systematically.

faq.question: Can this help with A/B testing setup?
faq.answer: This skill generates test hypotheses and specific alternatives to test, but it does not set up or run A/B tests. Pair this with an A/B testing skill to implement the recommended experiments.

faq.question: What page types does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, blog posts with CTAs, demo request pages, and resource/library pages. It also references related skills for signup flows, forms, and popups.

faq.question: How do I measure if recommendations work?
faq.answer: Track your primary conversion metric before and after implementing changes. Run changes as A/B tests when possible. Focus on statistically significant results over at least one full business cycle. Common metrics include conversion rate, click-through rate, and goal completions.
